dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8734 IH: To express the Sense of Congress with respect to safety of medication abortion and Federal preemption of State restrictions on dispensing medication abortion, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Ryan (for himself, Mrs. Fletcher, Mr. Vasquez, Ms. Chu, Ms. Ross, Ms. Norton, Ms. Titus, and Ms. Ansari) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To express the Sense of Congress with respect to safety of medication abortion and Federal preemption of State restrictions on dispensing medication abortion, and for other purposes.

section.header: Sense of Congress regarding safety of medication abortion and Federal preemption of State restrictions on dispensing medication abortion
section.text: It is the Sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: medication abortion is an extremely safe medical treatment that the Commissioner of Food and Drugs, based on scientific evidence, appropriately approved under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 301 et seq.); and
such approval under such Act preempts any State law establishing, implementing, or enforcing—

subparagraph.text: any requirement that medication abortion be dispensed by a health care practitioner in person; or
any prohibition or restriction on prescribing or dispensing medication abortion by means of telehealth.
